data.body:
G4 (child consistency) container review — parent epic `5fd2` × child `acc1` (Pass-2 verifier): NO FINDINGS (clean pairing).

Audited all 7 cross-child interaction modes for this child against the WHOLE sibling roster; high-confidence + benign-reading filter applied. Each mode resolves consistent-by-design.

1. IMPLICIT SHARED STATE — none unowned. Pass-2's outputs (severity attributes + typed binary sub-answers incl. the `cited_reference_accurate` veto) flow into a shared pipeline, but ownership is cleanly partitioned: (a) Pass-2 PRODUCES the attributes/sub-answers; Pass-3 (`487d`) CONSUMES them (validity = graded fraction, impact = ordinal mean, veto); no double-write. (b) the per-finding `id` is minted by `ca03` (sole owner) at post-Pass-3 assembly — acc1's schema carries no `id`. (c) Pass-2's DISCRIMINATION eval cases are the one cross-child shared artifact, and the sharing is EXPLICITLY coordinated: acc1's own AC4 places them "wired into the `7284` eval suite as Pass-2's OWN cases, distinct from the per-criterion prompt cases," and 7284's description + its G4 review both confirm "acc1's Pass-2 discrimination cases land in 7284." No uncoordinated shared state.

2. CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS — examined the strongest candidate and it is DESIGNED, not contradictory: acc1 (Pass-2) frames itself as "charitable-plan-reading (the finding stands only if it holds under the plan's most reasonable reading = evidence-entailment)," while sibling `1913` (Pass-1) frames itself as "skeptical-of-plan / grounding-not-charity / thorough (precision is Pass-2's job)." These are the two halves of the parent's deliberate division of labor (parent: "Pass 1 surfaces grounded FINDINGS … precision is Pass-2's job"; "Pass 2 … charitable plan-reading"). Each child states its own half correctly — complementary, not in conflict (this is the same near-candidate already cleared on the 1913×parent G4 pairing). Benign-reading filter: PASS.

3. DEPENDENCY GAP — none. The Pass-1→Pass-2→Pass-3 ordering is coherent and present: acc1 takes Pass-1's (`1913`) evidence records as CLAIMS-TO-TEST (independence); `487d` consumes acc1's attributes/sub-answers; `ca03` orchestrates the sequence (`pass2_verify` wired at orchestrator.py:638-639 per the completion record). acc1 carries no `depends_on` edge, but its one cross-ticket coordination (AC4's "wired into 7284") is backstopped by 7284's side (which CONSUMES the Pass-2 cases and whose completion-verifier PASS confirms they landed) — no missing breaking edge.

4. SCOPE OVERLAP — none. acc1's slice (Pass-2 PRODUCTION: one aggregate verify on a non-frontier model → severity attributes + typed binary sub-answers + charitable-reading entailment gate) does not overlap any sibling: the Pass-3 deterministic DECISION (veto→validity→impact→priority→block|advisory|dropped) is `487d`'s; the eval CASES for Pass-2 are `7284`'s (acc1's AC4 explicitly marks them "distinct from the per-criterion prompt cases" + "wired into 7284"); Pass-1 finding PRODUCTION is `1913`'s; the orchestration/wiring is `ca03`'s; the sidecar recording of validity/impact is `db7b`'s. acc1 produces the verification signal; each adjacent concern has a distinct named owner. No double-build.

5. ORDERING VIOLATION — none. Per the parent's risk-ordered MVP build order, acc1 (Pass-2) is in the thin vertical slice (DET floor 012e + registry/orchestrator ca03 + ONE single-turn criterion + Pass-1/2/3 1913/acc1/487d + attestation 4bb7 + claim 092b). Pass-2 follows Pass-1 and precedes Pass-3 — the correct dependency order.

6. CONSUMER IMPACT — none. acc1's output shape is consumed downstream without contradiction: `487d` consumes the five severity attributes (deterministic ordinal→[0,1] mean = impact) + the eight binary sub-answers (graded fraction = validity; `cited_reference_accurate` = the only veto); `db7b`'s `_slim` records validity/impact/priority/verification for offline analysis (read-only). acc1's `_pass2_model` vocabulary (SeverityAttrs {prod/debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility}; Binary {is_verifiable, evidence_entails_finding, path_reachable, impact_follows_necessarily, no_viable_alternative_explanation, no_existing_mitigation, severity_claim_justified} + the cited-reference veto) matches the parent's "Pass-2 vocabulary (validated against criteria_v8)" EXACTLY — no drift between the parent contract and the child's emitted schema.

7. RESIDUAL REFERENCES — none. acc1 references no removed/renamed sibling artifact: `7284` (eval suite), `E4`/`G1G2`/`A1` (the live code-grounding bright-line criteria its Scope defers grounding to), and the parent's Pass-2 vocabulary all resolve to live, named siblings/contracts. The verifier ships as a library prompt (`plan_review_verifier.md`) via the da27 machinery (ca03's lane) — no dangling pointer.

NOTE (NOT a G4 finding against THIS pairing): the parent AC's "agentic/tool-using for codebase-grounded findings and single-turn otherwise" Pass-2 dual-mode dispatch is unpinned by any acc1 AC — but that is a COVERAGE (G3) matter (a parent-AC element with no asserting AC), already recorded on this pairing's G3 review. For G4 it is NOT a contradiction: acc1's Scope ("code-citation findings are rare in plan review; grounding owned by E4/G1G2/A1") is CONSISTENT with the parent's agent-vs-single-turn bright line, and the child's completion comments confirm the dual mode WAS implemented ("agentic (tool-using) when any finding is code-grounded"). So acc1's Scope does not CONTRADICT the parent or any sibling — it states the same bright line from the other side; the unpinned-AC concern is coverage, not consistency, and surfaces only under G3.

NET: clean for G4 — this child is consistent with the parent and every sibling it interacts with across all 7 modes; the one examined near-candidate (skeptical-vs-charitable Pass-1/Pass-2 framing) is an intentional, parent-specified division of labor. Findings list is EMPTY.
